title: Deploy ollama-bridge to GKE — enable native `travel --provider ollama` on the production cluster
description: GKE currently has no `ollama` or `ollama-bridge` service deployed. `travel --provider ollama` on GKE falls back to OpenAI per the item #11 round's smoke. This round deploys ollama-bridge to GKE so the chooser pattern is fully populated across all four providers. The bridge service already exists in helm templates (`templates/ollama-bridge-deployment.yaml` + `templates/ollama-bridge-service.yaml` per the helm chart) — the question is whether to deploy it standalone or with an actual Ollama backend. Investigation phase decides: bridge-only (cheap), bridge + CPU Ollama (small models), or bridge + GPU node pool (full inference). Cap: depends on the decision; document it explicitly.

design_brief_for_codex.current_state: GKE has no ollama-bridge deployment. Travel runtime workload defaults `ollama_bridge_url` to `http://ollama-bridge.noetl.svc.cluster.local:8765/jsonrpc` — the service simply doesn't exist on GKE. Local kind has the bridge running. The bridge translates MCP JSON-RPC to Ollama's REST API; it needs an actual Ollama instance behind it to serve real inference.
design_brief_for_codex.decision_to_make:
Three deployment options, decide before implementing:
  (A) Bridge-only — deploy ollama-bridge without an actual Ollama backend. Useful for routing tests but `--provider ollama` will return errors when the bridge can't reach Ollama. Lowest cost, lowest value.
  (B) Bridge + CPU Ollama — small models (gemma3:4b, the existing default). Slow inference but real. ~2-4GB memory per Ollama pod. No GPU needed.
  (C) Bridge + GPU node pool — full inference, larger models, low latency. Requires provisioning a GPU node pool in the GKE cluster (cost implications).

Default recommendation: (B) — matches local kind's setup (CPU Ollama), enables real `--provider ollama` smokes, no GPU provisioning required. Cost: ~$30-50/month for one always-on Ollama pod.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_1_decide_and_inspect: Read the existing helm templates for ollama-bridge-deployment + ollama-bridge-service. Identify what the local kind setup looks like (image tags, resource requests, env vars). Decide option (A/B/C) and document.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_2_deploy_to_gke: Apply the chosen deployment to GKE. For option (B): deploy Ollama + ollama-bridge with the same image tags as kind. Pull a model (e.g. `ollama pull gemma3:4b` inside the Ollama pod) to warm the model cache.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_3_smoke: `travel --provider ollama help` on GKE — expected: effective_provider='ollama' (NOT the OpenAI fallback). Run the activities path too. Capture execution_id + audit row.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_4_close_out: Memory entry. Sync issue update (close the 'ollama on GKE' deferred line). Validation log append if GREEN. ai-meta pointer bump only if a helm value or new manifest was needed.

1_decide_and_inspect.objective: Decide option A/B/C; inspect existing helm/ops setup.
spec: Inspect local kind's running ollama deployment + bridge pod.
Pick option A/B/C with rationale. Default to (B) unless something blocks.

2_deploy_to_gke.objective: Deploy the chosen configuration to GKE.
spec: For (B): kubectl apply or helm upgrade with ollama + bridge enabled. Confirm both pods healthy.
Pull the default model inside the Ollama pod (`ollama pull gemma3:4b` or via kubectl exec).
Verify bridge can reach Ollama: `kubectl exec ollama-bridge -- curl -s ollama-service:11434/api/tags`.
2_deploy_to_gke.verify: Bridge + Ollama pods healthy; model loaded.

spec: `travel --provider ollama flights from SFO to JFK on 2026-07-15 for 2 adults` — same.
`travel --provider ollama activities near Times Square` — same (verifies the engine hydration handles ollama envelope too).

4_close_out.objective: Close-out memory + sync issue + validation log.
spec: Memory entry naming the deployed option (A/B/C) + resource cost.
Append validation log paragraph if GREEN.
Bump repos/ops gitlink only if helm values changed.

denied_tools: Provisioning GPU nodes in GKE without explicit Kadyapam approval (cost implication) — if option (C) is chosen, AMBER + STOP for user approval
Modifying the noetl S3 backend or storage tier (orthogonal concern)
preferred_tools: Browser screenshot for the gateway terminal smoke if appropriate
result_file_instructions: Write bridge/outbox/20260511-220000-ollama-bridge-gke-deployment.result.json. Required fields: {id, from: 'codex', to: 'claude-cowork', completed_at, overall_status, phases: {1_decide_and_inspect: {option_chosen, rationale}, 2_deploy_to_gke: {pods_healthy, model_loaded}, 3_smoke: {help_status, flights_status, activities_status, all_effective_provider_ollama: bool}, 4_close_out: {sync_issue_updated, memory_entry_path, validation_log_appended}}, verdict, notes, deferred_followups: ['Cloud-tier router decision', 'Amadeus production API switch']}.
